title: Add image upload and multiple servers icon to attack path visualizer

requirements.text: Add the ability to load and display user-provided images or pictures within the attack path diagram canvas

quotes: add a feature where I can load an image or picture in the diagram

acceptanceCriteria: Users can upload image files (PNG, JPG, JPEG) from their local system
Uploaded images appear as placeable elements on the attack path canvas
Images can be repositioned, resized, and deleted like other diagram elements
Images persist when diagrams are saved and loaded
Image upload interface is accessible from the attack path toolbar

requirements.text: Add a multiple servers icon to the attack path visualizer toolbar for representing server clusters or multiple server instances
acceptanceCriteria: A new 'multiple servers' icon type is available in the attack path toolbar
The icon visually represents multiple servers (e.g., stacked servers or server cluster)
The icon can be dragged onto the canvas like existing icon types
The icon integrates with existing connection, selection, and manipulation features

requirements.text: Update the backend DiagramState type to support storing uploaded image data including image content and metadata
acceptanceCriteria: DiagramState schema includes a new field for uploaded images with position, dimensions, and image data
Image data is stored efficiently (base64 or blob format)
Existing diagram save/load operations handle image data correctly

constraints: Images must be stored within the DiagramState backend structure
Use existing AttackPathCanvas component patterns for image manipulation
Maintain compatibility with existing diagram save/load functionality
Follow the single-actor backend architecture in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: External image hosting or CDN integration
Vector graphics or SVG editing tools
Image compression or optimization beyond basic formats

required: Multiple servers icon illustration showing stacked or clustered servers in a simple, clean style matching existing attack path icons (multiple-servers.dim_128x128.png)
